title: Annotate edit
description: Structured output contract for /annotate — translates a browser-side pin (user marked up a rendered preview) into a concrete code edit instruction for Claude. Each record describes ONE pin and its requested transformation.

pin_id.description: Stable id for the annotation so the same pin survives multiple critique rounds.

viewport.description: Viewport the pin was placed on. Required so the mapping from visual coordinates to CSS selector stays correct when the pin was made at mobile vs desktop.

selector.description: CSS selector that locates the pinned element in the source. Preferred form: data-testid or semantic tag. Free-form selector is accepted but less stable.

bounding_box.description: Pixel-space location of the pin at capture time. Populated by the browser-side annotator; used for visual diffing and as a fallback when selector matching fails.

instruction.description: One-sentence edit request in the user's own words. Short enough to fit into a subsequent critique round without bloating the prompt.

intent.description: Coarse classification of the edit to help the code-edit agent choose the right strategy.

evidence_screenshot.description: Path to the screenshot that was annotated. Round-trippable back into Playwright for visual diffing after the fix lands.
